# Mini AI
A simple character-level Markov chain generator.
from .markov import MarkovChain, train, generate, to_dict, from_dict

import argparse
from .markov import MarkovChain, train, generate, to_dict, from_dict

parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest='command')

train_parser = subparsers.add_parser('train', help='Train the model and save it to a file.')
train_parser.add_argument('--input', required=True, type=str)
train_parser.add_argument('--model-out', required=True, type=str)
train_parser.add_argument('--order', default=1, type=int)

@train_parser.command()
def train(args):
    model_path = args.model_out
    text = open(args.input).read().strip()
    train(text, model_path)

generate_parser = subparsers.add_parser('generate', help='Generate text using the trained model.')
generate_parser.add_argument('--model', required=True, type=str)
generate_parser.add_argument('--length', default=10, type=int)
generate_parser.add_argument('--seed', type=str)
generate_parser.add_argument('--random-seed', type=int)

@generate_parser.command()
def generate(args):
    model_path = args.model
    text = open(model_path).read().strip()
    mc = from_dict(json.loads(text))
    result = mc.generate(length=args.length, seed=args.seed, random_seed=args.random_seed)
    print(result)

notes: Add PEP8 compliance and test cases for MarkovChain and CLI functionality.
